title: Decrement --
subtitle: Decrease a value by one
goal: Learn how to use the -- operator to decrease a variable's value.
explanation: The -- operator decreases a variable's value by exactly one.
It is shorthand for x = x - 1.
Decrement is often used to count lives, time, or number of attempts.
After the operator runs, the variable holds the new value.
It is one of JavaScript's basic operators.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine a game where you lose one life after each mistake. That is how the -- operator works.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: You cannot write 10--. The -- operator works only with variables.

quiz.question: What value will lives have after lives-- if lives starts at 3?
